titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 725

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=962 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 11:41:10.574784+00 | translation moa=963 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 11:41:10.563588+00

p.cl: Eadwardus de lastalrich omnibus ad quos presentes litere peruenerint salutem .

Sciatis me dedisse et concessisse et presenti carta confirmasse deo et sancte Marie et sancte ebbe et herberto priori et monachis de col
dingham  duos toftos in eiemuthe . et unum toftum in terra mea de leth  in liberam et perpetuam ele
mosinam . tenendos in perpetuum de me et heredibus meis . reddento mihi et heredibus meis annuatim pro recog
nitione tres teisas de lacio serico . ad penthecosten . Quare uolo quod predicti monachi habeant et pos
sideant predictos toftos libere et quiete ab omni alio seruicio et consuetudine et exactione .
Edward of Restalrig, to all to whom the present letters come, sends greeting.

Know me to have given, granted, and by the present charter to have made firm, to God, Saint Mary, Saint Ebbe, and Herbert the prior and the monks of Coldingham, two tofts in Eyemouth, and one toft in my land of Leith, in free and everlasting alms; to be held of me and my heirs for ever, rendering to me and my heirs annually as recognition three taises of silk lace at Pentecost. Wherefore I will that the aforesaid monks have and possess the aforesaid tofts free and quit from all service, custom, and exaction.
With these witnesses, Romfar and Hervey chaplains, Waldef, Adam, Elias of Prenderguest, Gilbert the priest, William son of Hugh, Robert and Waldef son of William, Thurstin Tricotin, and many others.
